sutta: monks there are these 4 bases of sympathy but for generosity or charity kind speech doing a good turn and treating all alike these are the 4 are these 4 basis of sympathy and if we practice them then we can get a lot of friends there are there's a mention some other suta where some of the buddhas lay disciples and they had a great following of friends you know and because they practice these 4 things now first 1 is generosity you know nobody likes a selfish person nobody likes to go near a selfish person but you are generous everybody will want to come around you second 1 is kind speech to be uh be careful not that we speak kind words not words which are too hurting sometimes you can't help it now sometimes we have to speak a bit harsh words sometimes if you want to wake up some people just like sometimes parents have to spank their kids in a little while once in a while to get them to go along the right road right third 1 is doing a good turn doing a good turn to others and there's a suta elsewhere i think i might have gone through it the buddha said that it's very hard to find somebody who will do a good turn to somebody first normally we only return something that's good done to us you know but to be the first to do uh good to somebody else is quite rare just like a grateful person very difficult to find in the world and then the fourth 1 is treating all the line that means you don't discriminate too much especially in terms of race or in terms of wealth etc [Music] so these are the 4 basis of sympathy generosity kind speech doing a good turn and treating all alike now the next 2 times
